  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "To tailor the help, which scope should I prioritize: (A) sentence-level writing polish only, (B) argument and evidence check (logical consistency and evidence–claim alignment) with light wording fixes, (C) section-level reorganization and contribution framing, or (D) novelty/scholarship positioning versus prior work (e.g., Guo et al.)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "This section stakes a contribution claim for CALIGRA, so reframing the thesis and gap can clarify what is being claimed at a conceptual level.",
    "R1.2": "The chain from theoretical foundation to conclusions needs internal logical coherence within the section.",
    "R1.3": "The reported ECE values and their comparisons must align with the stated interpretations and conclusions.",
    "R1.5": "The section compresses motivation, method, theory, results, and discussion, so ordering and narrative flow at the subsection level may need adjustment.",
    "R1.6": "Sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, tone, and concision will improve readability and precision of claims.",
    "RX.1": "Calibration and generalization statements should include appropriate uncertainty qualifiers.",
    "RX.2": "Claims should avoid overgeneralization beyond the presented datasets, methods, and theory.",
    "RX.3": "Work should remain confined to assisting with this specific section rather than expanding scope to new experiments or models.",
    "RX.4": "Overstating calibration performance in agent settings could encourage unsafe decisions, so conservative phrasing is important.",
    "RX.5": "Any guidance should maintain traceability to the reported numbers, datasets, and cited prior work."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: To tailor the help, which scope should I prioritize: (A) sentence-level writing polish only, (B) argument and evidence check (logical consistency and evidence–claim alignment) with light wording fixes, (C) section-level reorganization and contribution framing, or (D) novelty/scholarship positioning versus prior work (e.g., Guo et al.)?

rationale.R1.1: This section stakes a contribution claim for CALIGRA, so reframing the thesis and gap can clarify what is being claimed at a conceptual level.
rationale.R1.2: The chain from theoretical foundation to conclusions needs internal logical coherence within the section.
rationale.R1.3: The reported ECE values and their comparisons must align with the stated interpretations and conclusions.
rationale.R1.5: The section compresses motivation, method, theory, results, and discussion, so ordering and narrative flow at the subsection level may need adjustment.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, tone, and concision will improve readability and precision of claims.
rationale.RX.1: Calibration and generalization statements should include appropriate uncertainty qualifiers.
rationale.RX.2: Claims should avoid overgeneralization beyond the presented datasets, methods, and theory.
rationale.RX.3: Work should remain confined to assisting with this specific section rather than expanding scope to new experiments or models.
rationale.RX.4: Overstating calibration performance in agent settings could encourage unsafe decisions, so conservative phrasing is important.
rationale.RX.5: Any guidance should maintain traceability to the reported numbers, datasets, and cited prior work.
